sections.heading: RT
sections.content:
Role: RT plays a supportive role in the treatment of PM. It is not routinely used as primary treatment but can be considered as sequential therapy after systemic therapy and/or P/D in select patients with stage I epithelioid histology. RT is an effective palliative treatment for relief of chest pain, bronchial or esophageal obstruction, or other symptomatic sites. Prophylactic RT is not routinely recommended to prevent instrument-tract recurrence.

Principles:
- Recommendations regarding RT should be made by radiation oncologists with experience in managing pleural mesothelioma
- The best timing for delivering RT after surgical intervention and/or in conjunction with chemotherapy should be discussed by a multidisciplinary team
- PET scanning for treatment planning can be used as indicated
- Prophylactic RT is not routinely recommended to prevent instrument-tract recurrence after pleural intervention
- Pleural IMRT after P/D in the presence of an intact lung may be considered in centers with experience, given the technical difficulty of this treatment
- Advanced technologies may be used such as IGRT, SRS, SBRT, and IMPT
- Special attention should be paid to minimize radiation to the contralateral lung as the risk of fatal pneumonitis with IMRT is excessively high when strict limits are not applied
- Extensive elective nodal irradiation (ENI) is not recommended

Dose Frameworks:
- Palliative - Chest wall pain from recurrent nodules: total_dose 20-40 Gy or 30 Gy, dose_per_fraction 4 Gy or 3 Gy, fractions 5-13 or 10, schedule 1-2 weeks or 2 weeks
- Palliative - Multiple brain or bone metastases: total_dose 30 Gy, dose_per_fraction 3 Gy, fractions 10, schedule 2 weeks
- Post P/D - Higher dose to higher risk areas: total_dose 45-60 Gy, dose_per_fraction 1.8-2 Gy, fractions 25-33, schedule 5-6 weeks

Approaches:
- Pleural IMRT: 45-60 Gy in 1.8-2 Gy fractions, concurrent chemo may be used, sequential therapy after systemic and/or P/D, fatal pneumonitis risk if limits not maintained
- Palliative RT: 20-40 Gy in fractions >=4 Gy or 30 Gy in 3 Gy fractions, for symptom relief
- SBRT/SRS: varies, for oligometastatic disease

Contraindications/Warnings: Prophylactic RT not routinely recommended. Fatal pneumonitis with IMRT if contralateral lung MLD <8.5 Gy not maintained. For post P/D RT: ipsilateral lung MLD <21 Gy, V20 <40%, contralateral lung V20 <7%, MLD <8 Gy.

Volume Guidelines: GTV includes gross tumor and clips. CTV for adjuvant RT after P/D includes entire pleural surface, clips, potential residual sites. PTV considers motion and setup errors. ENI not recommended.

timeline.body: Total dose 20-40 Gy or 30 Gy, dose per fraction 4 Gy or 3 Gy, fractions 5-13 or 10, schedule 1-2 weeks or 2 weeks
